title: brassica monensis subsp. cheiranthos
intro: Brassica monensis subsp. cheiranthos, also known as wild rocket, is a wild plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as other essential minerals, offering numerous health benefits including improved digestion, immune system support, and reduced risk of chronic diseases. Brassica monensis subsp. cheiranthos can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used as a raw ingredient in salads and other culinary dishes.
